devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अथो सुराः प्रत्युपलब्धचेतसः परस्य पुंसः परयानुकम्पया । जघ्नुर्भृशं शक्रसमीरणादयस्तांस्तान्रणे यैरभिसंहताः पुरा ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca atho surāḥ pratyupalabdhacetasaḥ parasya puṃsaḥ parayānukampayā jaghnurbhṛśaṃ śakrasamīraṇādayastāṃstānraṇe yairabhisaṃhatāḥ purā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Then, having recovered their minds (and morale from the impact of demonic illusions) through the great divine grace of the Supreme Person, Indra, Vāyu and other gods severely hit back each one of the demons who massively assailed them before, in that very battle.

devanagari: वैरोचनाय संरब्धो भगवान्पाकशासनः । उदयच्छद्यदा वज्रं प्रजा हा हेति चुक्रुशुः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: vairocanāya saṃrabdho bhagavānpākaśāsanaḥ udayacchadyadā vajraṃ prajā hā heti cukruśuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [aorist active second single]

translation: When the glorious Indra, being enraged at Bali, the son of Virocana, raised up his Vajra (thunderbolt), all creatures cried out, “Alas! Ha Ha” (lest it should kill Bali).

devanagari: वज्रपाणिस्तमाहेदं तिरस्कृत्य पुरःस्थितम् । मनस्विनं सुसम्पन्नं विचरन्तं महामृधे ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: vajrapāṇistamāhedaṃ tiraskṛtya puraḥsthitam manasvinaṃ susampannaṃ vicarantaṃ mahāmṛdhe
translation: Despising contemptuously the noble-minded, well- equipped Bali who was moving (fearlessly) in the great battle facing Indra, Indra—the wielder of Vajra, spoke to him as follows:

devanagari: नटवन्मूढ मायाभिर्मायेशान्नो जिगीषसि । जित्वा बालान्निबद्धाक्षान्नटो हरति तद्धनम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: naṭavanmūḍha māyābhirmāyeśānno jigīṣasi jitvā bālānnibaddhākṣānnaṭo harati taddhanam
translation: “Just as a roguish conjuror makes gullible persons as if blind-folded by his conjuring tricks (and incantations) and robs them of their money, you desire to conquer us, the masters of such Māyās (black magic and illusory tricks) with your Māyā, Oh fool.

devanagari: आरुरुक्षन्ति मायाभिरुत्सिसृप्सन्ति ये दिवम् । तान्दस्यून्विधुनोम्यज्ञान्पूर्वस्माच्च पदादधः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ārurukṣanti māyābhirutsisṛpsanti ye divam tāndasyūnvidhunomyajñānpūrvasmācca padādadhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ukṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ukṣ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ ukṣ class 1 verb]

translation: Those who by their illusive powers (or by unfaithful cunning observances of vows) wish to ascend to the celestial world or desire to transcend it to go to still higher regions (viz. Liberation), I shall oust such ignorant rogues from the post occupied by them previously and definitely throw them downward still (with no hopes of rising up).

devanagari: सोऽहं दुर्मायिनस्तेऽद्य वज्रेण शतपर्वणा । शिरो हरिष्ये मन्दात्मन्घटस्व ज्ञातिभिः सह ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: so śiro hariṣye mandātmanghaṭasva jñātibhiḥ saha
translation: Oh dull-witted fellow! I, that very Indra, shall, with my Vajra (thunderbolt) of hundred joints, remove the head of yours, so adept in black magic and evil spells. Show your valorous prowess with the help of your clansmen”.

devanagari: श्रीबलिरुवाच । सङ्ग्रामे वर्तमानानां कालचोदितकर्मणाम् । कीर्तिर्जयोऽजयो मृत्युः सर्वेषां स्युरनुक्रमात् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībaliruvāca saṅgrāme vartamānānāṃ kālacoditakarmaṇām kīrtirjayo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Bali responded
“To those whose previous acts became fruitful through the impelling of Time (Destiny) who participate in the battle, fame, victory, defeat and death come all in due course.

devanagari: तदिदं कालरशनं जगत्पश्यन्ति सूरयः । न हृष्यन्ति न शोचन्ति तत्र यूयमपण्डिताः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tadidaṃ kālaraśanaṃ jagatpaśyanti sūrayaḥ na hṛṣyanti na śocanti tatra yūyamapaṇḍitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

translation: Therefore wise, thoughtful people realize that all the above (victory, defeat etc.) are controlled by Time and they neither gst elated with joy or downcast with grief. You all are, however, ignorant about this (truth).

devanagari: न वयं मन्यमानानामात्मानं तत्र साधनम् । गिरो वः साधुशोच्यानां गृह्णीमो मर्मताडनाः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: na vayaṃ manyamānānāmātmānaṃ tatra sādhanam giro vaḥ sādhuśocyānāṃ gṛhṇīmo marmatāḍanāḥ

translation: Inasmuch as you regard yourself as the agent of this (victory, defeat etc.), you deserve to be pitied by good people (for your foolishness) and as such, we do not accept your words (howsoever cutting to the quick (they be)”.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्याक्षिप्य विभुं वीरो नाराचैर्वीरमर्दनः । आकर्णपूर्णैरहनदाक्षेपैराह तं पुनः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityākṣipya vibhuṃ vīro nārācairvīramardanaḥ ākarṇapūrṇairahanadākṣepairāha taṃ punaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having thus retorted to the Lord of gods, the valorous Bali, the vanquisher of warriors, struck again with Nārāca arrows drawn to the ear, Indra who was in retaliation previously hit back with reproachful words.

devanagari: एवं निराकृतो देवो वैरिणा तथ्यवादिना । नामृष्यत्तदधिक्षेपं तोत्राहत इव द्विपः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ nirākṛto devo vairiṇā tathyavādinā nāmṛṣyattadadhikṣepaṃ totrāhata iva dvipaḥ
translation: The god Indra who was thus reproached by the enemy who expressed (nothing but) the truth, did not however tolerate the retort like an elephant pricked with a goad.

devanagari: प्राहरत्कुलिशं तस्मा अमोघं परमर्दनः । सयानो न्यपतद्भूमौ छिन्नपक्ष इवाचलः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: prāharatkuliśaṃ tasmā amoghaṃ paramardanaḥ sayāno nyapatadbhūmau chinnapakṣa ivācalaḥ
translation: Indra, the conqueror of enemies, discharged at him, his unfailing weapon Vajra (thunderbolt). And down fell Bali on the ground, along with his (aerial) car like a mountain with its wings severed.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
In the Vālmīki Rāmāyaṇa Sundara 1.124-33 we arc told that in the Kṛta age mountains had wings which Indra clipped. Only Maināka was saved by Vāyu in repayment of which obligation he allowed Hanumān to rest during his jump to Laṅkā and told him of the clipping.

devanagari: सखायं पतितं दृष्ट्वा जम्भो बलिसखः सुहृत् । अभ्ययात्सौहृदं सख्युर्हतस्यापि समाचरन् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sakhāyaṃ patitaṃ dṛṣṭvā jambho balisakhaḥ suhṛt abhyayātsauhṛdaṃ sakhyurhatasyāpi samācaran

[genitive single from √ han class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ han class 2 verb]
[genitive single from √ han class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Seeing his friend fallen down, Jambha, the friend and well-wisher of Bali, attacked Indra with a view to carry out his duty as a friend, even though his friend was fallen.

devanagari: स सिंहवाह आसाद्य गदामुद्यम्य रंहसा । जत्रावताडयच्छक्रं गजं च सुमहाबलः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa siṃhavāha āsādya gadāmudyamya raṃhasā jatrāvatāḍayacchakraṃ gajaṃ ca sumahābalaḥ
translation: Riding his lion, the extremely powerful Jambha approached Indra and raising his mace (gadā) impetuously struck him on the neck and the elephant as well.

devanagari: गदाप्रहारव्यथितो भृशं विह्वलितो गजः । जानुभ्यां धरणीं स्पृष्ट्वा कश्मलं परमं ययौ ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: gadāprahāravyathito bhṛśaṃ vihvalito gajaḥ jānubhyāṃ dharaṇīṃ spṛṣṭvā kaśmalaṃ paramaṃ yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vyath class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vyath ]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Stunned and tormented by the stroke of the mace, the elephant was deeply distressed and touching the ground with its knees, it fell into a swoon.

devanagari: ततो रथो मातलिना हरिभिर्दशशतैर्वृतः । आनीतो द्विपमुत्सृज्य रथमारुरुहे विभुः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tato ratho mātalinā haribhirdaśaśatairvṛtaḥ ānīto dvipamutsṛjya rathamāruruhe vibhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: Thereupon, a chariot yoked with a thousand horses was brought by Mātali. The Lord of gods left the elephant and got into the chariot.

devanagari: तस्य तत्पूजयन्कर्म यन्तुर्दानवसत्तमः । शूलेन ज्वलता तं तु स्मयमानोऽहनन्मृधे ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya tatpūjayankarma yanturdānavasattamaḥ śūlena jvalatā taṃ tu smayamāno

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pūj class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ pūj class 10 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [imperfect active third plural]

translation: Expressing his appreciation at the (prompt) services- rendered by the Charioteer (Mātali) smiling, Jambha, the most prominent Dānava, struck him with a burning javelin.

devanagari: सेहे रुजं सुदुर्मर्षां सत्त्वमालम्ब्य मातलिः । इन्द्रो जम्भस्य सङ्क्रुद्धो वज्रेणापाहरच्छिरः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sehe rujaṃ sudurmarṣāṃ sattvamālambya mātaliḥ indro jambhasya saṅkruddho vajreṇāpāharacchiraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Screwing up all his fortitude, Mātali bore up the extremely unbearable pain of the dart. Greatly enraged at it, Indra sundered off the head of Jambha with his Vajra (thunderbolt).

devanagari: जम्भं श्रुत्वा हतं तस्य ज्ञातयो नारदादृषेः । नमुचिश्च बलः पाकस्तत्रापेतुस्त्वरान्विताः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: jambhaṃ śrutvā hataṃ tasya jñātayo nāradādṛṣeḥ namuciśca balaḥ pākastatrāpetustvarānvitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Having heard from Sage Nārada, the report of Jambha being killed (in the battle), his kinsmen Namuci, Bala, Pāka rushed to the battle field in great haste.

devanagari: वचोभिः परुषैरिन्द्र मर्दयन्तोऽस्य मर्मसु । शरैरवाकिरन्मेघा धाराभिरिव पर्वतम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: vacobhiḥ paruṣairindra mardayanto śarairavākiranmeghā dhārābhiriva parvatam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ mṛd ], [vocative plural from √ mṛd ]

translation: Cutting Indra to the quick by biting, harsh words, they covered him with volleys of arrows as clouds would do to a mountain, with a down-pour of rain.

devanagari: हरीन्दशशतान्याजौ हर्यश्वस्य बलः शरैः । तावद्भिरर्दयामास युगपल्लघुहस्तवान् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: harīndaśaśatānyājau haryaśvasya balaḥ śaraiḥ tāvadbhirardayāmāsa yugapallaghuhastavān
translation: In that battle, the light-handed Bala struck simultaneously all the thousand horses of Indra with as many arrows.

devanagari: शताभ्यां मातलिं पाको रथं सावयवं पृथक् । सकृत्सन्धानमोक्षेण तदद्भुतमभूद्रणे ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: śatābhyāṃ mātaliṃ pāko rathaṃ sāvayavaṃ pṛthak sakṛtsandhānamokṣeṇa tadadbhutamabhūdraṇe
translation: It was certainly an achievement of a marvellous feat in the battle, when Pāka simultaneously fitted and discharged two hundred arrows and struck Mātali with a hundred and all the parts of the chariot with a hundred shafts.

devanagari: नमुचिः पञ्चदशभिः स्वर्णपुङ्खैर्महेषुभिः । आहत्य व्यनदत्सङ्ख्ये सतोय इव तोयदः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: namuciḥ pañcadaśabhiḥ svarṇapuṅkhairmaheṣubhiḥ āhatya vyanadatsaṅkhye satoya iva toyadaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: With fifteen big arrows furnished with golden hilts, Namuci shot Indra and roared in the battle like a thundering rain-charged cloud.

devanagari: सर्वतः शरकूटेन शक्रं सरथसारथिम् । छादयामासुरसुराः प्रावृट्सूर्यमिवाम्बुदाः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvataḥ śarakūṭena śakraṃ sarathasārathim chādayāmāsurasurāḥ prāvṛṭsūryamivāmbudāḥ
translation: Like clouds obscuring the sun in the rainy season, the Asuras screened off on all sides Indra, along with his chariot and charioteer, with net-works of arrows.

devanagari: अलक्षयन्तस्तमतीव विह्वला विचुक्रुशुर्देवगणाः सहानुगाः । अनायकाः शत्रुबलेन निर्जिता वणिक्पथा भिन्ननवो यथार्णवे ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: alakṣayantastamatīva vihvalā vicukruśurdevagaṇāḥ sahānugāḥ anāyakāḥ śatrubalena nirjitā vaṇikpathā bhinnanavo yathārṇave

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural]

translation: Not being able to behold Indra and (considering themselves as) being leaderless and vanquished by the inimical forces, the hosts of celestials and their followers became bewildered and shrieked out like merchants in a ship wrecked on the high seas.

devanagari: ततस्तुराषाडिषुबद्धञ्जराद्विनिर्गतः साश्वरथध्वजाग्रणीः । बभौ दिशः खं पृथिवीं च रोचयन्स्वतेजसा सूर्य इव क्षपात्यये ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tatasturāṣāḍiṣubaddhañjarādvinirgataḥ sāśvarathadhvajāgraṇīḥ babhau diśaḥ khaṃ pṛthivīṃ ca rocayansvatejasā sūrya iva kṣapātyaye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ ruc ], [vocative single from √ ruc ]
[nominative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation: Thereupon issued out Indra along with his chariot, horses, flag and charioteer from the cage of arrows. Indra appeared brilliant like the sun illuminating the cardinal points, the sky and the earth, with his effulgence, at the close of the night.

devanagari: निरीक्ष्य पृतनां देवः परैरभ्यर्दितां रणे । उदयच्छद्रि पुं हन्तुं वज्रं वज्रधरो रुषा ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: nirīkṣya pṛtanāṃ devaḥ parairabhyarditāṃ raṇe udayacchadri puṃ hantuṃ vajraṃ vajradharo ruṣā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yam class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ yam class 1 verb]

translation: Observing his army overpowered by enemies in the battle, the god, the wielder of Vajra (thunderbolt) vehemently lifted up his thunderbolt (determined) to strike down the enemy.

devanagari: स तेनैवाष्टधारेण शिरसी बलपाकयोः । ज्ञातीनां पश्यतां राजन्जहार जनयन्भयम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tenaivāṣṭadhāreṇa śirasī balapākayoḥ jñātīnāṃ paśyatāṃ rājanjahāra janayanbhayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ jan ], [vocative single from √ jan ]

translation: With that very eight-edged weapon he (simultaneously) sundered the heads of Bala and Pāka, striking terror into the hearts of the kinsmen who stood (simply) looking on, Oh King (Parīkṣit).

devanagari: नमुचिस्तद्वधं दृष्ट्वा शोकामर्षरुषान्वितः । जिघांसुरिन्द्रं नृपते चकार परमोद्यमम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: namucistadvadhaṃ dṛṣṭvā śokāmarṣaruṣānvitaḥ jighāṃsurindraṃ nṛpate cakāra paramodyamam

translation: On perceiving their death and being overwhelmed with grief, revenge and rage, Namuci, determined to kill Indra, put forth a mighty exertion.

devanagari: अश्मसारमयं शूलं घण्टावद्धेमभूषणम् । प्रगृह्याभ्यद्र वत्क्रुद्धो हतोऽसीति वितर्जयन् । प्राहिणोद्देवराजाय निनदन्मृगराडिव ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: aśmasāramayaṃ śūlaṃ ghaṇṭāvaddhemabhūṣaṇam pragṛhyābhyadra vatkruddho hato prāhiṇoddevarājāya ninadanmṛgarāḍiva

translation: Catching hold of a javelin made of iron, furnished with gold, he attacked Indra in rage, threatening loudly, “Now you are killed”. He hurled it at the King of gods and roared like a lion.

devanagari: तदापतद्गगनतले महाजवं विचिच्छिदे हरिरिषुभिः सहस्रधा । तमाहनन्नृप कुलिशेन कन्धरे रुषान्वितस्त्रिदशपतिः शिरो हरन् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tadāpatadgaganatale mahājavaṃ vicicchide haririṣubhiḥ sahasradhā tamāhanannṛpa kuliśena kandhare ruṣānvitastridaśapatiḥ śiro haran

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Even as it was dashing with terrific speed through the sky, Indra shattered it into thousand pieces with his arrows. The lord of the celestials, out of rage, struck him with his Vajra (thunderbolt) on the neck with a view to chop off his head.

devanagari: न तस्य हि त्वचमपि वज्र ऊर्जितो बिभेद यः सुरपतिनौजसेरितः । तदद्भुतं परमतिवीर्यवृत्रभित्तिरस्कृतो नमुचिशिरोधरत्वचा ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: na tasya hi tvacamapi vajra ūrjito bibheda yaḥ surapatinaujaseritaḥ tadadbhutaṃ paramativīryavṛtrabhittiraskṛto namuciśirodharatvacā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ūrj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ūrj class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ ūrj class 7 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The thunderbolt that was discharged with great vehemence did not pierce his skin. It was certainly the most wonderful that the Vajra (the thunderbolt) that killed the extremely powerful Vṛtra, was baffled by the skin of Namuci’s neck.

devanagari: तस्मादिन्द्रो ऽबिभेच्छत्रोर्वज्रः प्रतिहतो यतः । किमिदं दैवयोगेन भूतं लोकविमोहनम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmādindro kimidaṃ daivayogena bhūtaṃ lokavimohanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Indra apprehended fear from the enemy from whom even Vajra (thunderbolt) was baffled. What has happened through the Destiny, has deluded the whole of the world.

devanagari: येन मे पूर्वमद्रीणां पक्षच्छेदः प्रजात्यये । कृतो निविशतां भारैः पतत्त्रैः पततां भुवि ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: yena me pūrvamadrīṇāṃ pakṣacchedaḥ prajātyaye kṛto niviśatāṃ bhāraiḥ patattraiḥ patatāṃ bhuvi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pakṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pakṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pakṣ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pat class 1 verb]

translation: It is by this thunderbolt that, in days of yore, I carried out the severance of wings of mountains which taking to their wings at will, settled anywhere on the earth, causing destruction to people, by their weight.

devanagari: तपःसारमयं त्वाष्ट्रं वृत्रो येन विपाटितः । अन्ये चापि बलोपेताः सर्वास्त्रैरक्षतत्वचः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tapaḥsāramayaṃ tvāṣṭraṃ vṛtro yena vipāṭitaḥ anye cāpi balopetāḥ sarvāstrairakṣatatvacaḥ
translation: It was by this Vajra that Vṛtra, the very embodiment of the power of the austere penance of Tvaṣṭṛ, was split asunder, and also other mighty (persons) whose skin could not be scratched by all other missiles.

devanagari: सोऽयं प्रतिहतो वज्रो मया मुक्तोऽसुरेऽल्पके । नाहं तदाददे दण्डं ब्रह्मतेजोऽप्यकारणम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: so nāhaṃ tadādade daṇḍaṃ brahmatejo
translation: Such Vajra, when hurled by me at an insignificant Asura, became baffled and ineffective. Useless has become the spiritual energy of the Brāhmaṇa (with which it was invested). It has become (nothing but) a stick which I won’t handle any more”.

devanagari: इति शक्रं विषीदन्तमाह वागशरीरिणी । नायं शुष्कैरथो नाद्रैर्वधमर्हति दानवः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śakraṃ viṣīdantamāha vāgaśarīriṇī nāyaṃ śuṣkairatho nādrairvadhamarhati dānavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ śuṣ class 6 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ śuṣ class 6 verb]

translation: To Indra who was thus pondering in dejection, a voice from heaven said, “This demon should not be killed by weapons wet or dry.

devanagari: मयास्मै यद्वरो दत्तो मृत्युर्नैवार्द्र शुष्कयोः । अतोऽन्यश्चिन्तनीयस्ते उपायो मघवन्रिपोः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: mayāsmai yadvaro datto mṛtyurnaivārdra śuṣkayoḥ ato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive dual from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [locative dual from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [genitive dual from √ śuṣ class 6 verb], [locative dual from √ śuṣ class 6 verb]
[genitive dual from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [locative dual from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [genitive dual from √ śuṣ class 6 verb], [locative dual from √ śuṣ class 6 verb]
[genitive dual from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [locative dual from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [genitive dual from √ śuṣ class 6 verb], [locative dual from √ śuṣ class 6 verb]

translation: He has been conferred a boon by me that he would not meet death from wet or dry things. Hence, you have to think out some other expedient (for killing) the enemy”.

devanagari: तां दैवीं गिरमाकर्ण्य मघवान्सुसमाहितः । ध्यायन्फेनमथापश्यदुपायमुभयात्मकम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ daivīṃ giramākarṇya maghavānsusamāhitaḥ dhyāyanphenamathāpaśyadupāyamubhayātmakam
translation: Hearing the heavenly voice, Indra composed his mind and contemplated. It occurred to him (lit. he saw) that foam (of the sea) was the (real) expedient which comprises both the conditions (of being wet and dry).

devanagari: न शुष्केण न चाद्रेर्ण जहार नमुचेः शिरः । तं तुष्टुवुर्मुनिगणा माल्यैश्चावाकिरन्विभुम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: na śuṣkeṇa na cādrerṇa jahāra namuceḥ śiraḥ taṃ tuṣṭuvurmunigaṇā mālyaiścāvākiranvibhum

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [instrumental single from √ śuṣ class 6 verb]
[instrumental single from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [instrumental single from √ śuṣ class 6 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative single from √ ir class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ ir class 6 verb]

translation: With the foam which was neither dry nor wet (but was both simultaneously) he chopped off the head of Namuci.[2] Hosts of sages sang his eulogies and showered the Lord of gods with flowers.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
apāṃ phenena namuceḥ śira indrodavartayaḥ Ṛg Veda Saṃhitā 8.14.13, AV. 20.29.3.

devanagari: गन्धर्वमुख्यौ जगतुर्विश्वावसुपरावसू । देवदुन्दुभयो नेदुर्नर्तक्यो ननृतुर्मुदा ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: gandharvamukhyau jagaturviśvāvasuparāvasū devadundubhayo nedurnartakyo nanṛturmudā
translation: The chief of Gandharvas, Viśvāvasu and Parāvasu gave musical concert. Gods beat big drums and celestial nymphs danced with joy.

devanagari: अन्येऽप्येवं प्रतिद्वन्द्वान्वाय्वग्निवरुणादयः । सूदयामासुरसुरान्मृगान्केसरिणो यथा ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: anye sūdayāmāsurasurānmṛgānkesariṇo yathā
translation: Other gods like Vāyu, Agni. Varuṇa and others destroyed their opponents with volleys of missiles, just as lions would kill the deer.

devanagari: ब्रह्मणा प्रेषितो देवान्देवर्षिर्नारदो नृप । वारयामास विबुधान्दृष्ट्वा दानवसङ्क्षयम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaṇā preṣito devāndevarṣirnārado nṛpa vārayāmāsa vibudhāndṛṣṭvā dānavasaṅkṣayam
translation: The divine sage Nārada who was deputed to celestials by god Brahmā, on perceiving the slaughter of Dānavas, prevented them (from that genocide, with the following words):

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । भवद्भिरमृतं प्राप्तं नारायणभुजाश्रयैः । श्रिया समेधिताः सर्व उपारमत विग्रहात् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvāca bhavadbhiramṛtaṃ prāptaṃ nārāyaṇabhujāśrayaiḥ śriyā samedhitāḥ sarva upāramata vigrahāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Nārada said:
“Supported as you were by the arms of Lord Nārāyaṇa, nectar was secured by you. You are also favoured by goddess Śrī (the consort of Nārāyaṇa). Hence you now cease from fighting.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । संयम्य मन्युसंरम्भं मानयन्तो मुनेर्वचः । उपगीयमानानुचरैर्ययुः सर्वे त्रिविष्टपम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca saṃyamya manyusaṃrambhaṃ mānayanto munervacaḥ upagīyamānānucarairyayuḥ sarve triviṣṭapam

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Paying due respect to the words of the sage Nārada, they controlled the impetuosity of their rage. All of them returned to heaven, all the while being praised by their attendants.

devanagari: येऽवशिष्टा रणे तस्मिन्नारदानुमतेन ते । बलिं विपन्नमादाय अस्तं गिरिमुपागमन् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: ye baliṃ vipannamādāya astaṃ girimupāgaman

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third plural], [aorist active third plural]

translation: The survivors of the battle carried the still unconscious Bali to the western mountain (of the setting sun) as per Nārada’s advice.

devanagari: तत्राविनष्टावयवान्विद्यमानशिरोधरान् । उशना जीवयामास संजीवन्या स्वविद्यया ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāvinaṣṭāvayavānvidyamānaśirodharān uśanā jīvayāmāsa saṃjīvanyā svavidyayā
translation: Śukrācārya (the preceptor of Daityas) restored to life (all the Daityas) whose limbs were intact, and necks in existence (unbroken), by means of his secret science of restoring life (saṃjīvani vidyā).

devanagari: बलिश्चोशनसा स्पृष्टः प्रत्यापन्नेन्द्रियस्मृतिः । पराजितोऽपि नाखिद्यल्लोकतत्त्वविचक्षणः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: baliścośanasā spṛṣṭaḥ pratyāpannendriyasmṛtiḥ parājito
translation:
Bali regained his consciousness and other faculties at the touch of Śukra’s hand. Being endowed with rhe reality about the nature of the world, Bali suffered no depression, even though he was defeated.
